item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
short and sweet not over done i like it.
would like to see it being tested though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why hello there, fellow sheep.

It feels a little bit lackluster and has room for improvements, but I like the drive behind it. The themes are a little rough around the edges and could use some improvements, but I do enjoy articles that use the anomaly as more of a plot device of a story than a focus.

And besides, you can scarcely go wrong with an ovine!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He actually isn't experienced at all. I don't know if I made it clear enough, but the poacher group is literally like Parawatch, they do almost nothing and are utterly unprepared for anything anomalous. Schmid's "big discovery" was probably something like a picture of bigfoot. They're all pretty stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also don't really care for the character here. I know he's meant to be blinded by jealousy, but it still beggars belief that anyone would see something that explodes, and is immune to bullets, and think that stabbing it is a good solution, let alone someone experienced in hunting anomalous animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The jealousy was definitely apparent, though not to a level that made me feel any more than minor distaste towards the character. This may have been due to the short length of the article, since hatred is a pretty strong feeling that's difficult to properly evoke. I'm also feeling that the attempted comedy of the article really didn't help make me hate this character, as I was mentally prepared for something more lighthearted right from the conprocs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On the point of the character, you were meant to feel no sympathy towards them, and even maybe hate, or be glad that he's dead. He was blinded by jealousy towards Schmid, so he was willing to do anything to one-up whatever he was planning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When stressed, SCP-5033 will go into a 5033-IGUSHA event.

"When stressed" is an incredibly vague condition. Even just giving a few examples of what could cause this event would have improved this line.

Besides that, I just wasn't really attached to the character. I was confused to some extent as to how I was meant to feel, since they're clearly trying to prove they are worth something, but then they're poaching and trying to kill animals so I felt no sympathy for them. The comedy didn't land especially well either, as I knew how it was going to conclude already and nothing was done with the dramatic irony besides playing out the clearest, most obvious situation of him just assuming it would all be fine.

item.title: response
item.content:encoded: I really like the containment procedures, and that it feels like a joke skip, but at the same time more of a mainlister with the wording.

item.title: this is a self insert
item.content:encoded:
My jamcon entry for the first theme, Explosions!

Thank you to my critters:

  • cybersqyd
  • Oboebandgeek99
  • XilasCrowe
  • swordlover87
  • Gekkoguy
